zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert I de Coucy於大約1109年出生在法國皮卡迪索姆波福斯時，他的父親Thomas de Marle-Coucy37歲，母親Mélisende de Crécy27歲。他與Beatrix de Saint Pol結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他去世於1191年7月26日在巴勒斯坦منطقة الشمالعكّا享年83歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert I de Coucy于大约1109年出生在法国皮卡迪索姆波福斯时，他的父亲Thomas de Marle-Coucy37岁，母亲Mélisende de Crécy27岁。他与Beatrix de Saint Pol结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。他去世于1191年7月26日在巴勒斯坦منطقة الشمالعكّا享年83岁。

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

nameMeanings.name: Coucy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of Arabic   Khoury   or   Khouri   ‘priest’. Compare   Courey  ,   Couri  , and   Cury  .
French: habitational name from a place so named in Vienne.

variants.name: Khoury
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Nabil, Sami, Samir, Issa, Ibrahim, Ramzi, Bassam, Fouad, Habib, Issam, Jamil, Riad.  French Michel, Antoine, Emile, Pierre, Andre, Camille, Clemence, Clovis, Georges, Gisele, Henri, Jacques.

Arabic (mainly Lebanon, Syria, and Jordan): from khūrī ‘priest’, in particular a Christian priest. This surname is used among Christians, including Assyrians/Chaldeans. Compare Cory , Coury , Cury , Elkhoury , Khouri , Kory , Koury , and Kury .

en.lifeSummary: When Robert I de Coucy was born about 1109, in Boves, Somme, Picardie, France, his father, Thomas de Marle-Coucy, was 37 and his mother, Mélisende de Crécy, was 27. He married Beatrix de Saint Pol. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. He died on 26 July 1191, in Acre, Northern, Palestine, at the age of 83.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert I de Coucy est né environ 1109, à Boves, Somme, Picardie, France, son père, Thomas de Marle-Coucy, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Mélisende de Crécy, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Beatrix de Saint Pol. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Il est décédé le 26 juillet 1191, à Acre, Northern, Palestine, à l’âge de 83 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert I de Coucy um 1109 in Boves, Somme, Picardie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas de Marle-Coucy 37 und seine Mutter Mélisende de Crécy 27. Er hat Beatrix de Saint Pol geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Er starb am 26. Juli 1191 im Alter von 83 Jahren in Acre, Northern, Palästina.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert I de Coucy è nato il circa 1109 nel Boves, Somme, Picardie, Francia, il suo padre, Thomas de Marle-Coucy, aveva 37 anni e il suo madre, Mélisende de Crécy, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Beatrix de Saint Pol. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì il 26 luglio 1191, nel Acre, Northern, Palestina, all'età di 83 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert I de Coucyが約1109年にフランスピカーディソンメボベスで生まれた時，父Thomas de Marle-Coucyは37歳，母Mélisende de Crécyは27歳であった。Beatrix de Saint Polと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼は1191年7月26日にパレスチナمنطقة الشمالعكّاで83歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert I de Coucy(이)가 대략 1109년 프랑스 피카디 솜 보브스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas de Marle-Coucy(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Mélisende de Crécy(은)는 27세였다. 그는 Beatrix de Saint Pol(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1191년 7월 26일 팔레스타인 منطقة الشمال عكّا에서 83세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert I de Coucy nasceu aproximadamente 1109, em Boves, Somme, Picardie, França, seu pai, Thomas de Marle-Coucy, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Mélisende de Crécy, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Beatrix de Saint Pol. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ele faleceu em 26 de julho de 1191, em Acre, Northern, Palestina, com 83 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert I de Coucy родился около 1109 г. в местности бовесь, сомм, пикарди, Франция, его отцу по имени Thomas de Marle-Coucy было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Mélisende de Crécy, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Beatrix de Saint Pol. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Он умер 26 июля 1191 г. в местности عكّا, منطقة الشمال, Палестина в возрасте 83 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert I de Coucy nació aproximadamente 1109, en Boves, Somme, Picardie, Francia, su papá, Thomas de Marle-Coucy, tenía 37 años y su mamá, Mélisende de Crécy, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Beatrix de Saint Pol. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió el 26 de julio de 1191, en Acre, Northern, Palestina, a la edad de 83 años.
